content.question: How do parental decision-making and self-exclusion mechanisms in the admissions process respond to changes in transparency, cost, or accessibility of exam preparation, and would admissions-system reform alone reduce segregation absent broader equity measures in ordinary schools?
content.reduction_path: Design a field experiment or quasi-experimental analysis varying the accessibility, cost, or marketing of exam preparation among disadvantaged families, and measure changes in application rates, pass rates, and school composition; compare results across schools with different track structures to isolate the effect of admissions reform from ambient school-quality differences.
